title: Phase 8 sidecar — security controls
description: Structured security-controls catalog the Phase-8 LLM persists at PHASE_END. Consumed by scripts/build_threat_model_yaml.py to populate threat-model.yaml[security_controls[]] without re-composing in Phase 11. Field names mirror the output schema (threat-model.output.schema.yaml) so the aggregator can pass entries through verbatim. Augments .architecture-coverage.json[control_assessments] — sidecar wins on conflict (LLM has more context). See docs/substep2-deterministic-migration.md §2.5.

security_controls.description: Catalog of controls with effectiveness ratings. Drives §7 Operational Strengths and §2.4 layer tables. Field names match threat-model.output.schema.yaml security_controls[] verbatim.

domain.description: Architectural-layer or control-family label (e.g. 'Identity and Authentication Controls', 'Data Protection', 'Network Segmentation'). Free-form string per output schema — no enum.

control.description: Specific control implementation (e.g. 'JWT Authentication', 'Password Hashing', 'TLS termination').

effectiveness.description: Implementation/effectiveness rating. Adequate = good; Partial = covers some surfaces; Weak = present but flawed (e.g. RS256 with hardcoded key); Missing = control absent.

kind.description: Optional control kind for §7 grouping. When absent, the deterministic pregenerator falls back to architectural-controls.yaml lookup.

assessment.description: Free-form prose explaining the rating. Cites file:line where applicable.

linked_threats.description: T-IDs of threats related to this control. Cross-ref validated by aggregator — orphan T-IDs surface as advisory warnings.
